templates.name: Xandao Interfaces by SNMP
templates.description:
Template Module Interfaces

MIBs used:
IF-MIB

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n' then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000' to`n+499,999'.  For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accurate estimation can be made, this object should contain the nominal bandwidth. For a sub-layer which has no concept of bandwidth, this object should be zero.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is ready to transmit and receive network traffic
- It should change todormant(5) if the interface is waiting for external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an incoming connection)
- It should remain in the down(2) state if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going to the up(1) state
- It should remain in the notPresent(6) state if the interface has missing(typically, hardware) components.

trigger_prototypes.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Link down
trigger_prototypes.description:
This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1` - a user can redefine context macro to value - 0. That marks this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `{TEMPLATE_NAME:METRIC.diff()}=1` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, do not fire for the 'eternal off' interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

trigger_prototypes.expression:
change(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])<0 and last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0
and (
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=6 or
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=7 or
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=11 or
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=62 or
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=69 or
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=117
)
and
(last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])<>2)
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression:
(change(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0 and last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2)>0) or
(last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2)
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Ethernet has changed to lower speed than it was before
trigger_prototypes.description: This Ethernet connection has transitioned down from its known maximum speed. This might be a sign of autonegotiation issues. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

dependencies.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Link down
dependencies.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))

trigger_prototypes.expression:
(avg(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.in[ifHCInOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)>({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}/100)*last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}]) or
avg(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.out[ifHCOutOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)>({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}/100)*last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])) and
last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression:
avg(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.in[ifHCInOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)<(({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}-3)/100)*last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}]) and
avg(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.out[ifHCOutOctets.{#SNMPINDEX}],15m)<(({$IF.UTIL.MAX:"{#IFNAME}"}-3)/100)*last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifHighSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): High bandwidth usage
trigger_prototypes.description: The utilization of the network interface is close to its estimated maximum bandwidth.

dependencies.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Link down
dependencies.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))

trigger_prototypes.expression:
min(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.in.errors[ifInErrors.{#SNMPINDEX}],5m)>{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"{#IFNAME}"}
or min(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.out.errors[ifOutErrors.{#SNMPINDEX}],5m)>{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"{#IFNAME}"}
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression:
max(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.in.errors[ifInErrors.{#SNMPINDEX}],5m)<{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"{#IFNAME}"}*0.8
and max(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.out.errors[ifOutErrors.{#SNMPINDEX}],5m)<{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"{#IFNAME}"}*0.8
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): High error rate
trigger_prototypes.description: It recovers when it is below 80% of the `{$IF.ERRORS.WARN:"{#IFNAME}"}` threshold.

dependencies.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Link down
dependencies.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/Xandao Interfaces by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))

templates.template: Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP
templates.name: Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP
templates.description:
Template Net Network Generic Device

MIBs used:
EtherLike-MIB
HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
SNMPv2-MIB
IF-MIB

Generated by official Zabbix template tool "Templator"

triggers.expression: max(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmpping,#3)=0
triggers.name: Unavailable by ICMP ping
triggers.description: Last three attempts returned timeout.  Please check device connectivity.

triggers.expression: min(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$ICMP_LOSS_WARN} and min(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100
triggers.name: High ICMP ping loss

dependencies.name: Unavailable by ICMP ping
dependencies.expression: max(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmpping,#3)=0

triggers.name: High ICMP ping response time
triggers.description: Average ICMP response time is too high.

dependencies.name: High ICMP ping loss
dependencies.expression: min(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)>{$ICMP_LOSS_WARN} and min(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmppingloss,5m)<100

dependencies.name: Unavailable by ICMP ping
dependencies.expression: max(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/icmpping,#3)=0

items.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
items.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

items.name: System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person.  If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name.  If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

triggers.expression: last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/system.name,#1)<>last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/system.name,#2) and length(last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/system.name))>0
triggers.name: System name has changed
triggers.description: The name of the system has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity.  This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example, if vendor`Flintstones, Inc.' was assigned the subtree1.3.6.1.4.1.4242, it could assign the identifier 1.3.6.1.4.1.4242.1.1 to its `Fred Router'.

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface,including framing characters. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and another times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system,
and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in bits per second.
For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accurate estimation can be made,
this object should contain the nominal bandwidth.
If the bandwidth of the interface is greater than the maximum value reportable by this object then
this object should report its maximum value (4,294,967,295) and ifHighSpeed must be used to report the interface's speed.
For a sub-layer which has no concept of bandwidth, this object should be zero.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interface is ready to transmit and receive network traffic
- It should change todormant(5) if the interface is waiting for external actions (such as a serial line waiting for an incoming connection)
- It should remain in the down(2) state if and only if there is a fault that prevents it from going to the up(1) state
- It should remain in the notPresent(6) state if the interface has missing(typically, hardware) components.

trigger_prototypes.expression: {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1 and last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2 and (last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression: last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])<>2 or {$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=0
trigger_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Link down
trigger_prototypes.description:
This trigger expression works as follows:
1. It can be triggered if the operations status is down.
2. `{$IFCONTROL:"{#IFNAME}"}=1` - a user can redefine the context macro to "0", marking this interface as not important. No new trigger will be fired if this interface is down.
3. `{TEMPLATE_NAME:METRIC.diff()}=1` - the trigger fires only if the operational status was up to (1) sometime before (so, does not fire for "eternal off" interfaces.)

WARNING: if closed manually - it will not fire again on the next poll, because of .diff.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

change(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])<0 and last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0
and (
last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=6 or
last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=7 or
last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=11 or
last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=62 or
last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=69 or
last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.type[ifType.{#SNMPINDEX}])=117
)
and
(last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])<>2)
trigger_prototypes.recovery_expression:
(change(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}])>0 and last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.speed[ifSpeed.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2)>0) or
(last(/Xandao Network Generic Device by SNMP/net.if.status[ifOperStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}])=2)

discovery_rules.name: EtherLike-MIB Discovery
discovery_rules.description: Discovering interfaces from IF-MIB and EtherLike-MIB. Interfaces with up(1) Operational Status are discovered.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Duplex status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: EtherLike-MIB
The current mode of operation of the MAC
entity.  'unknown' indicates that the current
duplex mode could not be determined.

Management control of the duplex mode is
accomplished through the MAU MIB.  When
an interface does not support autonegotiation,
or when autonegotiation is not enabled, the
duplex mode is controlled using
ifMauDefaultType.  When autonegotiation is
supported and enabled, duplex mode is controlled
using ifMauAutoNegAdvertisedBits.  In either
case, the currently operating duplex mode is
reflected both in this object and in ifMauType.

Note that this object provides redundant
information with ifMauType.  Normally, redundant
objects are discouraged.  However, in this
instance, it allows a management application to
determine the duplex status of an interface
without having to know every possible value of
ifMauType.  This was felt to be sufficiently
valuable to justify the redundancy.
Reference: [IEEE 802.3 Std.], 30.3.1.1.32,aDuplexStatus.
